data.content: PriceCalculator also needs a using. Easier to just enable implicit usings project-wide and revert the manual additions:

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-5d128c508e9b4d28a5d311ff838906fe/fixtures/modern-mstest/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-5d128c508e9b4d28a5d311ff838906fe/fixtures/modern-mstest/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-5d128c508e9b4d28a5d311ff838906fe/fixtures/modern-mstest/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-5d128c508e9b4d28a5d311ff838906fe/fixtures/modern-mstest/TestProject.csproj
@@ -2,6 +2,7 @@
 
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
     <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
   </PropertyGroup>

data.content:
All **10 tests pass**. Here's a summary of the test file created at `OrderServiceTests.cs`:

| Test | What it verifies |
|---|---|
| `CreateOrder_WithValidItems_ReturnsOrderWithCorrectTotal` | Total calculation, customer ID, item count, ID generation, timestamp |
| `CreateOrder_WithSingleItem_CalculatesTotalCorrectly` | Decimal arithmetic with one item |
| `CreateOrder_WithNullCustomerId_ThrowsArgumentNullException` | Null guard on customer ID |
| `CreateOrder_WithNullItems_ThrowsArgumentNullException` | Null guard on items list |
| `CreateOrder_WithEmptyItems_ThrowsInvalidOperationException` | Empty items validation |
| `GetOrder_AfterCreation_ReturnsMatchingOrder` | Round-trip create → retrieve |
| `GetOrder_WithNonExistentId_ReturnsNull` | Missing order returns null |
| `GetOrdersByCustomer_ReturnsOnlyMatchingOrders` | Filters by customer correctly |
| `GetOrdersByCustomer_WithNoOrders_ReturnsEmptyList` | Unknown customer → empty list |
| `CreateOrder_MultipleTimes_AssignsUniqueIds` | Each order gets a unique ID |

The tests follow modern MSTest patterns: `sealed` class, constructor initialization (no `[TestInitialize]`), `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>`, and descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` naming.
